mission.title: Install and Configure Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE)
mission.description: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated cloud services, but it may incur higher costs compared to self-managed options. Key trade-offs include ease of use versus potential vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Install the OCI CLI
steps.description:
The OCI CLI is required to interact with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. This step installs the CLI on your local machine.

```bash

curl -L https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cli/master/scripts/install/install.sh | bash

```

> This command downloads and runs the installation script.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in a designated region.

```bash

oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

```

> Replace <your_compartment_id> and <your_vcn_id> with your actual compartment and VCN IDs.
commands: oci ce cluster create --compartment-id <your_compartment_id> --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.30.2 --vcn-id <your_vcn_id> --node-pool-configuration '"nodePoolConfigurations": [{"name": "my-node-pool", "nodeShape": "VM.Standard2.1", "nodeCount": 3}]'

steps.title: Configure kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to interact with your new cluster.

```bash

oci ce cluster create-kubeconfig --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --file $HOME/.kube/config --region <your_region>

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> and <your_region> with your actual cluster ID and region.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up additional features like autoscaling and monitoring for your cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/oracle/oci-cloud-controller-manager/main/deploy/oci-cloud-controller-manager.yaml

kubectl create namespace monitoring

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/prometheus-operator/prometheus-operator/main/bundle.yaml -n monitoring

```

> This deploys the OCI Cloud Controller Manager.
> Create a namespace for monitoring.
> Deploy Prometheus for monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

oci ce cluster delete --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --force --wait-for-state TERMINATED

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.

uninstall.title: Remove the kubeconfig
uninstall.description:
This step removes the kubeconfig file to prevent further access to the deleted cluster.

```bash

rm $HOME/.kube/config

```

> This deletes the kubeconfig file.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. This command upgrades the cluster to the specified version.

```bash

oci ce cluster update --cluster-id <your_cluster_id> --kubernetes-version v1.31.0

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace <your_cluster_id> with your actual cluster ID.
> Verify that the nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running OCI CLI commands.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the CLI is not properly authenticated.

**Fix:**
Run 'oci setup config' to reconfigure your CLI credentials.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl get nodes returns 'NotReady'.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the nodes are still initializing or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the node status in the OCI console and ensure that the VCN and subnets are correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: OCI CLI command fails with 'InvalidParameter' error.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that one of the parameters provided in the command is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Double-check the parameters used in the command, especially IDs and names.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient quota' when creating a cluster.
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your OCI account has reached its resource limits.

**Fix:**
Review your OCI resource quotas and request an increase if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new node shapes and improved integration with OCI services.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools installed and configured before proceeding.
